mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are non-negative in >70% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic activity without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of quarters, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where INDPRO rises, or return autocorrelation fully explains the signal rather than the macro variable.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly additions to nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 signal strengthening household income and consumption capacity, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during recovery phases, or predictive power vanishes when controlling for prior month momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 120 percent of their twelve-month moving average indicate accelerating residential investment and construction sector momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >65% of triggered months, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal stagflationary pressures and aggressive monetary tightening, which compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals sudden supply-side contraction and deteriorating business conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for market volatility regimes.
